summary: Norway's posture is led by the National Security Authority (NSM), which runs a dedicated quantum migration programme and issues cryptographic recommendations advising organisations to plan and carry out migration to quantum-resistant cryptography. NSM identifies the algorithms in use today that quantum computers would break and has opened a Centre for Applied Cryptology to strengthen national quantum-safe preparedness.
algorithms: RSA, ECDH, ECDSA, Diffie-Hellman (named as quantum-vulnerable)
govActors:
NSM | Norwegian National Security Authority, runs the quantum migration programme and issues cryptographic recommendations
Research Council of Norway | Funds quantum computing strategy work referencing post-quantum cryptography
provenance: Lead institution + posture: NSM quantum migration section and cryptographic recommendations (multiple ingested NSM rows, incl. English-language version)
Vulnerable algorithms RSA/ECDH/ECDSA/Diffie-Hellman: NSM 'Vulnerable algorithms' row (these are the classical algorithms NSM names as quantum-vulnerable, not adopted PQC algorithms)
Centre for Applied Cryptology: NSM announcement row
Research Council of Norway: QCNorway project-bank entry (flagged included:false, listed as a minor actor only)
